channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Control of state House could hinge on a sleepy district outside Duluth
item.description: Control of the Minnesota House of Representatives could come down to one race in a district outside Duluth, where first-term Republican Natalie Zeleznikar is being challenged by Democratic retired judge Mark Munger.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: AI sector frustrated by Congress' slow pace keeping up with the advanced tech
item.description: People who work with the AI sector have said the industry is dissatisfied with the rate at which Congress is moving to keep pace with the advanced technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massachusetts town issues cease and desist to resident projecting political signs on town water tower
item.description: Massachusetts town official warns resident projecting political signs onto the town's municipal water tower to stop or face fines.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most AAPI adults think legal immigrants give the US a major economic boost: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: According to a new poll, Asian American, Native Hawaiian and Pacific Islander adults are more likely than the overall U.S. population to view legal immigration as an asset to the country’s economy and workforce.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's protests aside, his agenda has plenty of overlap with Project 2025
item.description: Donald Trump insists that Project 2025, a nearly 1,000-page blueprint for a hard-right turn in American government and society, does not reflect his priorities for a White House encore.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democratic voter registration raises red flags for Harris
item.description: Democrats’ voter registration advantage has dropped in three key battleground states — Pennsylvania, North Carolina and Nevada — raising a red flag for Vice President Harris as experts cite a lack of enthusiasm for the Biden administration brand and the Democratic Party, generally, as problems.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Former President Bill Clinton travels to Georgia to rally rural Black voters to the polls
item.description: ALBANY, Ga. (AP) — Former President Bill Clinton urged churchgoers in Albany, Georgia, on Sunday to rally behind Vice President Kamala Harris' campaign for the office he once held.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cringeworthy pro-Harris ad showcasing male support features professional actors
item.description: A new political video featuring men "man enough" to vote for Vice President Kamala Harris is stacked with actors from the LA area, a review of the cast shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance doesn't say where patients will get care if Planned Parenthood is defunded
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance (R), former President Trump's running mate, on Sunday didn't say where patients would go for health screenings, checkups and more if Planned Parenthood is defunded.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, NewsNation

item.title: US to deploy missile defense system, military personnel to Israel
item.description: President Biden approved the deployment of an advanced missile defense system to Israel along with roughly 100 U.S. troops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump suggests a protester may get 'the hell knocked out of her' by her parents
item.description: COACHELLA, Calif. (AP) — Former President Donald Trump called for a protester at one of his rallies to “go back home to Mommy” to “get the hell knocked out of her,” his latest instance of using violent language when confronted by demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz tramps through tall grass but bags no birds as pheasant hunting season opens
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz carried a shotgun and tramped through tall grass as Minnesota's pheasant hunting season opened, giving the campaign a chance to highlight the governor's rural roots and love of outdoor sports. Neither Walz nor Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inside the TikTok documents: Stripping teens and boosting ‘attractive’ people
item.description: A trove of secret documents show teens’ increasingly reliant on TikTok and how executives were acutely aware of the potential harm the app can cause young people, but appeared unconcerned.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, Sylvia Goodman, and Dara Kerr, MPR News

item.title: Trump hears at a Latino campaign event from someone who lived in the US illegally
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Minutes after Donald Trump delivered his standard warnings about drug dealers and criminals illegally crossing the border during a Saturday campaign event, the former president heard from someone who was once in the country illegally but now plans to vote for him.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fight for House raging in districts ringing NYC where Trump struggled but other Republicans thrived
item.description: NELSONVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — Voters in a ring of congressional districts encircling New York City where Republican candidates often do well but Donald Trump struggled in 2020 could decide which party controls the U.S. House for the next two years.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance points out the 'biggest difference' between leadership of Trump, Harris during campaign stop
item.description: At a campaign stop in Pennsylvania on Saturday, Sen. JD Vance called out VP Kamala Harris for her failures to America's homeless veterans during her tenure in the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris-Walz campaign targets hunters, fishermen for support
item.description: The Harris-Walz campaign launched a new coalition designed to draw voter support from outdoorsmen in swing states
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Key disaster loan program 'quickly exhausting' funds after hurricanes
item.description: Officials are warning a key federal program that provides disaster loans to businesses and homeowners is running critically low on funds in the aftermath of back-to-back hurricanes, increasing the pressure on Congress to return to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Comer slams Raskin over his election certification comments: 'Ultimate hypocrite'
item.description: House Oversight Chairman James Comer (R-Ky.) condemned comments from Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.), calling him “the ultimate hypocrite” after the Democrat claimed former President Trump is trying to manipulate the electoral college.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Jamie Dimon: 'Treacherous' geopolitical conditions getting worse
item.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon on Friday warned about potential risks in the future for the economy, citing the war in Ukraine and Israel's war against Hamas and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Senate candidates steer clear of debates
item.description: Senate candidates this cycle are largely steering clear of the debate stage, in some cases avoiding it altogether, marking a major change from past years as campaigns question the debates' importance. For years, it was common for top Senate races to feature multiple debates.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: House Democrats bet on small and fun events for minority outreach
item.description: The House Democratic campaign arm is increasingly relying on small, community-centered events for its get-out-the-vote efforts among voters of color, looking to increase interest with nonpolitical hooks.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's complaints about '60 Minutes' put a spotlight on editing at the nation's top newsmagazine
item.description: Donald Trump skipped a “60 Minutes” interview this past week, but he's attacking CBS for how it handled its session with opponent Kamala Harris, calling it a “giant fake news scam” and suggesting the network was out to protect her.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota’s ‘uncommitted’ voters go into election unsatisfied over lack of cease-fire, conflicted over Harris
item.description: Minnesota sent the most “uncommitted” delegates to the Democratic National Convention in protest of President Joe Biden’s stance toward Israeli’s military actions in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Republican NY-18 candidate Alison Esposito says America doesn’t have a gun problem
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Alison Esposito, a Republican candidate running for Congress in New York’s 18th District, said she does not support a ban on assault weapons and that America doesn’t have a gun problem. Esposito made the comments during a debate against Democratic U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Ryan Hamilton discusses homelessness, the Animal Foundation, and F1
item.description: Ryan Hamilton, a Republican candidate for the Clark County Commission, appeared on 8 News Now's Daily Voting Guide to share his perspective on homelessness, animal welfare, and Formula One's impact on the local economy as the election nears.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to watch the NY-18 Debate: Ryan vs. Esposito
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Candidates in the race for New York’s 18th Congressional District will face off in a debate Wednesday night. Democratic incumbent Congressman Pat Ryan will take on Republican challenger Alison Esposito in a 60-minute debate moderated by PIX11’s Henry Rosoff.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, PIX 11

item.title: Biden postpones trip to Germany and Angola to monitor Hurricane Milton
item.description: President Joe Biden said Tuesday he is postponing a planned trip to Germany and Angola to stay at the White House to monitor Hurricane Milton, as it spins closer to Florida’s Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Some Minneapolis Metro Transit trains will switch to buses starting Oct. 9
item.description: Part of the Metro Transit’s Blue and Green Lines will be closed starting at 10 p.m. on Wednesday for scheduled maintenance. It will reopen by 3 a.m. Monday, Oct. 14.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst, MPR News

item.title: Lombardo among GOP governors criticizing Biden-Harris immigration asylum actions
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo was among 25 Republican governors who signed a letter criticizing the Biden-Harris administration over asylum policies that have brought more than a half-million immigrants into the United States.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michael Naft discusses traffic safety, F1, and animal welfare in the Las Vegas valley
item.description: Michael Naft, incumbent county commissioner for District 8, joined Brian Loftis on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Monday, discussing his campaign priorities as election day nears.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pro-Trump Christian nationalists are on tour to recruit election workers
item.description: “Are you guys ready to show the world that Christians will be silent no more?” said one speaker, whose nonprofit is recruiting people to become election workers.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Hagen and Audrey Nguyen, MPR News

item.title: Major North Carolina newspaper knocks Trump over Helene response 'falsehoods'
item.description: A leading newspaper in North Carolina is blasting former President Donald Trump over what it calls his "falsehoods" about the government response to Hurricane Helene, which devastated large swaths of the state last week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: GOP senator calls Trump special counsel filing 'a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic'
item.description: Arkansas Sen. Tom Cotton (R) said in a Sunday interview that a recently unsealed filing from special counsel Jack Smith is “a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5
